title: Rosebank College Admission Requirements Without Matric 2026

content:
Rosebank College Admission Requirements Without Matric

Rosebank College (a brand of The Independent Institute of Education, IIE) is one of South Africa's most popular private colleges. A common question is: can you study at Rosebank College without a matric certificate? The answer is yes, in some cases. This guide explains all the options available to students without matric in 2026.

Can You Study at Rosebank College Without Matric?

Rosebank College offers Higher Certificate (NQF Level 5) programmes that do not require a full National Senior Certificate (matric/NSC). Students without matric can qualify through the following routes:

Option 1: N3 Certificate (TVET College)

  • An N3 certificate with relevant subjects from a TVET College may qualify you for some Higher Certificate programmes
  • The N3 must be completed with English and relevant technical/commercial subjects

Option 2: Mature Age Exemption (RPL)

  • Students who are 23 years or older may qualify through Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL)
  • Relevant work experience in the field of study is considered
  • You may need to complete a placement test or interview
  • Documentation of work experience required (reference letters, payslips, portfolio)

Option 3: Conditional Matric Results

  • If you have partial matric results (did not pass all subjects), contact Rosebank College directly
  • Some programmes may accept students on a conditional basis pending matric completion

Rosebank College Higher Certificate Programmes (No Full Matric Required)

ProgrammeDurationEntry Requirement
Higher Certificate in Business Management1 yearN3 or mature age exemption
Higher Certificate in Office Administration1 yearN3 or mature age exemption
Higher Certificate in Information Technology1 yearN3 or mature age exemption
Higher Certificate in Hospitality Management1 yearN3 or mature age exemption
Higher Certificate in Marketing1 yearN3 or mature age exemption
Higher Certificate in Supply Chain Management1 yearN3 or mature age exemption

Important: After completing a Higher Certificate, you can apply to continue into a Diploma or Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) at Rosebank College, giving you a complete pathway to a full degree.

Rosebank College Admission Requirements WITH Matric

For students who do have matric, Rosebank College generally requires:

  • National Senior Certificate (NSC): A minimum pass (usually a Bachelor's pass or Diploma pass, depending on the programme)
  • Specific subject requirements: Vary by programme — Business programmes typically require English and Mathematics or Mathematical Literacy
  • APS requirements: Generally lower than traditional universities (typically 20–28+ APS for diploma and degree programmes)
QualificationMin Matric RequirementTypical APS
Bachelor of Business AdministrationBachelor's Pass (NSC)24+
Diploma in Business ManagementDiploma Pass (NSC)20+
Diploma in Information TechnologyDiploma Pass (NSC)20+
Higher Certificate (all programmes)Any NSC pass or N3Not specified

How to Apply to Rosebank College 2026

  1. Visit the Rosebank College website: www.rosebankcollege.co.za
  2. Select your programme and campus (Johannesburg, Cape Town, Durban, Pretoria, Pietermaritzburg, East London, Polokwane)
  3. Submit your online application with certified copies of your ID and academic records
  4. Application fee: Approximately R300–R500 (check the current fee on the Rosebank College website)
  5. Receive an offer: Rosebank College typically responds within a few days to weeks

Rosebank College Fees 2026

Rosebank College is a private institution and fees are higher than public universities:

  • Higher Certificate: Approximately R40,000–R60,000 per year
  • Diploma programmes: Approximately R50,000–R70,000 per year
  • Bachelor's degrees (BBA): Approximately R60,000–R90,000 per year

NSFAS does NOT fund private institution students at Rosebank College. However, the NSFAS bursary for TVET colleges may apply if you are at a public TVET. Rosebank College has its own bursary and payment plan options — contact them directly for the most up-to-date financial aid information.

Is Rosebank College Worth It Without Matric?

Studying at Rosebank College through the Higher Certificate route can be a viable pathway to a career if:

  • You could not complete matric or failed subjects
  • You want to upskill or change careers
  • You want to progress to a diploma or degree later

After completing a Higher Certificate, you can continue studying at Rosebank College — the IIE qualifications are recognised for articulation into higher levels.

While at Rosebank College or preparing for admission, use StudyBuddy's AI tutor to strengthen your foundational skills in business, maths, and English.
keywords: can I study at Rosebank College without matric
quickAnswer: Yes, you can study at Rosebank College without a full matric certificate. Higher Certificate programmes are available for students with an N3 certificate from a TVET College, or through mature age exemption (RPL) for applicants 23 years or older with relevant work experience.

faqs.question: Can I study at Rosebank College without matric?
faqs.answer: Yes. Rosebank College accepts students without a full matric certificate for Higher Certificate programmes through two routes: (1) an N3 certificate from a TVET college with relevant subjects, or (2) mature age exemption (RPL) for applicants 23+ with relevant work experience.

faqs.question: What is the Higher Certificate at Rosebank College?
faqs.answer: Higher Certificate programmes at Rosebank College are 1-year NQF Level 5 qualifications in areas like Business Management, IT, Marketing, and Hospitality. They are designed for students who do not meet the requirements for a full diploma or degree. After completing a Higher Certificate, you can progress to a Diploma or BBA.

faqs.question: Does Rosebank College accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: No. NSFAS does not fund students at private institutions like Rosebank College (IIE). Rosebank College has its own payment plan and bursary options. Contact Rosebank College directly for their current financial aid options.

faqs.question: How do I apply to Rosebank College?
faqs.answer: Apply online at www.rosebankcollege.co.za. Select your programme and campus, submit certified copies of your ID and academic documents, and pay the application fee of approximately R300–R500. Rosebank College responds relatively quickly compared to public universities.
